item.title: [SAPD] Assault — 2500 GOLIAD RD (SOUTH)
item.description: [SAPD Calls for Service] Keyword: Assault — Incident #SAPD-2026-0991363 | SOUTH Division | 7/22/2026 5:53:27 AM

item.title: New review highlights gaps in care for First Nations children in Canada
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — A new study finds that First Nations children still face long-standing inequalities in health care access in Canada despite improvements made through the application of Jordan's Principle—a policy designed to ensure First Nations children receive the services they need. The work is published in the Journal of Indigenous Social Development.

item.title: AI agent went rogue, hacked AI startup Hugging Face: OpenAI
item.description: [AI & Automation] Keyword: AI agent — OpenAI called it an "unprecedented cyber incident" and pledged to support a joint investigation, with enewed attention to the risks of advanced artificial intelligence.

item.title: AI agent went rogue and hacked startup by itself, OpenAI reveals
item.description: [AI & Automation] Keyword: AI agent — Firm behind ChatGPT reveals agent powered by its models chose to attack Hugging Face database by itselfOpenAI has revealed an autonomous AI agent powered by its technology went rogue during a test, accessed the open web and hacked a prominent startup by itself in an “unprecedented incident”.The company behind ChatGPT said the startup Hugging Face had detected and contained the agent – an AI tool designed to carry out tasks without human assistance – which had entered its systems. Continue readin

item.title: A Tale of Two Behavioral Health Systems, or How the State Border Determines Who Gets Access to Mental Healthcare
item.description: [Cannabis, Hemp & Drug Policy] Keyword: THCA — In July 2025, the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) mandated significant health care funding cuts and policy changes related to low-income health insurance programs like Medicaid and the Children’s Health Insurance Program (CHIP) administration.  As of April 2026, 1.59 million Americans had lost their Medicaid insurance because of OBBBA-related cuts. Because of the bill, […] The post A Tale of Two Behavioral Health Systems, or How the State Border Determines Who Gets Access to Mental

item.title: Danish fish fossil cliffs set to gain UNESCO status
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Two Danish coastal cliffs where paleontologists have uncovered 55-million-year-old fossils of prehistoric wildlife are likely to gain World Heritage status as UNESCO's committee meets in South Korea.

item.title: Goats of football: Indian village holds tournament with an Indigenous twist
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — In a remote district surrounded by forests and hills, teams from tribal villages compete for football glory - and goats.

item.title: Rapid bacterial community profiling of equine microbiomes using Oxford Nanopore sequencing
item.description: [Science & Research] Keyword: microbiology — Members of the Horse Microbiome Research Group recently published a new study in the Journal of Medical Microbiology, led by Dr. Joy Leng, who is currently undertaking the Alborada Well Foal 2 project. This research aims to understand how the foal gut microbiome develops by investigating the microbiomes of mares and their foals.

item.title: OpenAI admits it was the source of the agent swarm that attacked Hugging Face
item.description: [Cybersecurity] Keyword: zero day — Sandboxed experiment found itself a zero day, escaped onto the open internet and validated scary predictions about rogue agents

item.title: Australia news live: Origin Energy investigating potential data breach that could affect millions
item.description: [Cybersecurity] Keyword: data breach — Follow today’s news liveGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastThe New South Wales premier, Chris Minns, says the state’s Labor party is under threat from One Nation and “we’ve got to fight for those votes”.Appearing on ABC Radio National this morning, Minns was asked if One Nation was a “racist party”. He said people who had concerns about the levels of immigration weren’t necessarily racist but some of the things Pauline Hanson had said were.Certainly some of the things Pau

item.title: Magic mushrooms may reshape the brain long after the trip ends
item.description: [Psychedelics & Psilocybin Research] Keyword: psilocybin — A single dose of psilocybin produced measurable changes in brain activity and possible changes in brain structure that lasted for up to a month. Participants who experienced the greatest increase in flexible, varied brain activity also reported more personal insight and later improvements in well-being. The findings suggest that the psychedelic experience itself may help people break free from entrenched patterns of thinking.

item.title: [SAPD] Missing Person/Runaway — 2200 PALOMINO (WEST)
item.description: [SAPD Calls for Service] Keyword: Missing Person/Runaway — Incident #SAPD-2026-0990007 | WEST Division | 7/21/2026 8:48:56 PM

item.title: Testing innovation speeds up detection of deadly tick-borne disease
item.description: [Science & Research] Keyword: microbiology — A new method at the University of Missouri could transform how Rocky Mountain spotted fever is diagnosed. The method detects the deadly tick-borne disease in just 40 minutes—a leap that could help patients get lifesaving treatment sooner. The study, "A rapid isothermal RPA–CRISPR/Cas12a assay for detection of Rickettsia rickettsii," was published in Frontiers in Microbiology.

item.title: Coffee may protect your heart but energy drinks could harm it
item.description: [Diabetes & Kidney Disease] Keyword: type 2 diabetes — For most adults, a daily caffeine habit may be safer for the heart than many people assume. A new American Heart Association review found that up to 400 milligrams of caffeine a day, roughly three to five cups of regular black coffee, is generally safe and may even be linked to lower risks of heart disease, stroke, heart failure, and Type 2 diabetes. But the source matters: highly concentrated energy drinks and shots can sharply raise blood pressure and trigger irregular heart rhythms.

item.title: More young women in their 20s are getting type 2 diabetes
item.description: [Diabetes & Kidney Disease] Keyword: type 2 diabetes — England is seeing "a worrying increase" in women in their 20s developing type 2 diabetes, say experts. Here's what to look out for.

item.title: Electyrolyzer experiment designated ready-to-fly by NASA
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — A Southwest Research Institute and University of Texas at San Antonio experiment has passed a critical NASA review, designating it ready for parabolic flight testing. The two institutions will evaluate the performance of a patent-pending electrolyzer, the Mars Atmospheric Reactor for Synthesis of Consumables (MARS-C), in partial-gravity environments.

item.title: Mayor Jones floats ending Ready to Work early to redirect funds to San Antonio’s budget shortfall
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — San Antonio faces a $158 million budget gap — and Mayor Gina Ortiz Jones is targeting a major city program funded through 2030 to close it.Mayor Jones floats ending Ready to Work early to redirect funds to San Antonio’s budget shortfall was first posted on July 21, 2026 at 9:02 pm.©2021 "San Antonio Report". Use of this feed is for personal non-commercial use only. If you are not reading this article in your feed reader, then the site is guilty of copyright infringement. Ple

item.title: Court Decision to Allow Nuclear Regulator to Ignore Climate Impacts on Future Reactor Operations Does Not Serve Public Safety
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — A unanimous decision by three US Court Of Appeals judges in the District Of Columbia to deny an appeal based on the risks of climate change on nuclear reactor safety is a shocking disregard for the ever worsening weather conditions that could put reactor communities at grave risk, said Beyond Nuclear today, one of the groups that brought the appeal.The petition denied by the court was brought by Beyond Nuclear and the Sierra Club, against the US Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC), and the Nucle

item.title: [SAPD] Assault In Progress — 8500 TOM SLICK (PRUE)
item.description: [SAPD Calls for Service] Keyword: Assault In Progress — Incident #SAPD-2026-0989197 | PRUE Division | 7/21/2026 5:38:23 PM

item.description: [SAPD Calls for Service] Keyword: Assault In Progress — Incident #SAPD-2026-0989203 | CENTRAL Division | 7/21/2026 5:39:34 PM

item.title: Students unearth rare, centuries-old pot at Florida Spanish mission site
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — Some 400 years ago, a member of the Indigenous Potano-Timucua tribe scooped up some clay and carefully molded it into a modest vessel, small enough to rest in the palm of their hand. You can imagine their fingers pressing a tight crease around the pot's simple rim and smoothing its dull gray surface, leaving it free of any adornment. We may never know exactly what the pot held or how many hands it passed through before it was excavated, still fully intact, by students at the University of Florid

item.title: Tapeworm hijacks worker ants, giving them queen-like metabolism and longer lives
item.description: [Science & Research] Keyword: genomics — A tapeworm fundamentally changes the lives of ants: Workers of the species Temnothorax nylanderi infected with the tapeworm Anomotaenia brevis live several times longer than their uninfected nestmates. They are also less active, and their metabolism becomes more similar in some respects to that of queens. A new study by Johannes Gutenberg University Mainz (JGU), published in the journal BMC Genomics, reveals the molecular basis of this unusual effect.

item.title: AI tool predicts how DNA sequences switch on genes across different cell types
item.description: [Science & Research] Keyword: genomics — Deep learning approaches have transformed how scientists predict the activity and function of DNA sequences in the genome. A new AI tool called Corgi (Context-aware Regulatory Genomics Inference), developed by Martin Vingron's bioinformatics group at the Max Planck Institute for Molecular Genetics allows researchers to predict how the genome behaves across different cell types. The findings could help improve efforts to mimic the activity of cells virtually, with potential applications in basic

item.title: A native California wildflower isn't keeping up with climate change
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — The mountain jewelflower inhabits a wide swath of California and Oregon, from summer-scorched foothills to high mountains where snow falls during winter. But it is already suffering from climate change, with plants in the mountains struggling to survive both summers and winters, according to a study from the University of California, Davis.

item.title: Republicans Are Trying to Give Big Oil Immunity for Causing Climate Chaos
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — A new report further links fossil fuel emissions to extreme weather events. States are trying to make polluters pay.

item.title: Open-source AI predicts key peptide traits before costly lab testing
item.description: [Diabetes & Kidney Disease] Keyword: GLP-1 — Penn Engineers have developed PeptiVerse, an AI-powered platform that predicts key chemical and biological properties of peptides, strings of amino acids whose medical potential has been demonstrated by the success of GLP-1 drugs, widely used weight-loss treatments.

item.title: First rescued beluga whales arrive at SeaWorld San Antonio from closed Canadian marine park
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — Two rescued beluga whales have arrived at SeaWorld San Antonio as the effort to relocate animals from Marineland of Canada begins.First rescued beluga whales arrive at SeaWorld San Antonio from closed Canadian marine park was first posted on July 21, 2026 at 5:19 pm.©2021 "San Antonio Report". Use of this feed is for personal non-commercial use only. If you are not reading this article in your feed reader, then the site is guilty of copyright infringement. Please contact me at hel

item.title: [SAPD] Assault In Progress — 6700 BELLA COLINA (PRUE)
item.description: [SAPD Calls for Service] Keyword: Assault In Progress — Incident #SAPD-2026-0988642 | PRUE Division | 7/21/2026 3:51:14 PM

item.title: Truffles are on the menu for threatened marsupials returning to a World Heritage area
item.description: [Ecology & Conservation] Keyword: ecosystem restoration — A new study from Edith Cowan University (ECU) confirms that the presence of fungal food resources is important for supporting the reintroduction of two threatened marsupials to the Shark Bay World Heritage area, which could simultaneously advance species conservation and ecosystem restoration. This study, led by Edith Cowan University Ph.D. candidate Rebecca Quah, took place at six sites across Western Australia and the Northern Territory, including Dirk Hartog Island in Shark Bay.

item.title: USAA pitches in $5 million for Alamo Plan to highlight San Antonio’s military community
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — USAA's contribution creates a resting area for veterans at the site, plus a mural to highlight San Antonio’s identity as Military City USA.USAA pitches in $5 million for Alamo Plan to highlight San Antonio’s military community was first posted on July 21, 2026 at 2:37 pm.©2021 "San Antonio Report". item.title: Hawaiian stilt reclassified from endangered to threatened after 50 years of conservation efforts
item.description: [Ecology & Conservation] Keyword: endangered species — The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service announced the reclassification of the Aʻeo, or Hawaiian stilt (Himantopus mexicanus knudseni), from endangered to threatened under the Endangered Species Act, a rare and hopeful milestone for a native Hawaiian species. The change, published in the Federal Register alongside a species-specific 4(d) rule, reflects more than 50 years of conservation work that has brought the long-legged waterbird back from the brink. The recovery is documented in a new study led b

item.title: Researchers call for a shift in crop development to tackle hunger, malnutrition, and climate change
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Feeding a growing global population is no longer enough. Scientists say the next generation of crops must also deliver better nutrition while withstanding the increasing impacts of climate change.

item.title: Extreme precipitation in Central Asia connected to drought in mainland Southeast Asia
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — In much of Asia, the effects of climate change are felt through an increased frequency of extreme weather events, especially water-related ones. Droughts are becoming more common in mainland Southeast Asia, while periods with unusually large amounts of rainfall are increasing in Central Asia. Rice University's Na Wang and Sylvia Dee were interested in understanding whether these two extremes were related, despite being geographically distant. The researchers' findings were recently published in

item.title: Excerpt: How to Close a Camp
item.description: [Immigration & Border] Keyword: immigration detention — Editor’s Note: The following is excerpted with permission from How to Close a Camp: Dispatches from the Fight Against Immigrant Detention, out from Haymarket Books this July. The Prison-Immigration Knot By the late 1980s, thanks to over a decade of laws criminalizing immigrants and expanding the camp system, prison and immigration detention industries were operating […] The post Excerpt: How to Close a Camp appeared first on The Texas Observer.

item.title: Trump fires warning shot at Canada: 'They need us to survive'
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — President Donald Trump once again took aim at Canada after reigniting his feud with America's neighbor to the north, claiming "they need us to survive" during an Oval Office event on Wednesday.Trump this week escalated his long-simmering feud with Canada after wildfires in the country blanketed much of the American Northeast and Midwest in dangerous levels of smoke, an occurrence becoming more common with the advance of climate change. Holding Canada responsible for the air quality threat, Trump

item.title: San Antonio filmmaker Jen Prince returns home with debut feature, message for women directors
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — Jen Prince, a 1994 Taft High School graduate, will premiere her first feature film "Miles Underwater" at the 32nd Annual San Antonio Film Festival Wednesday night downtown at the Radius Center. San Antonio filmmaker Jen Prince returns home with debut feature, message for women directors was first posted on July 21, 2026 at 11:27 am.©2021 "San Antonio Report". item.title: SAISD officially hires Adrian Bustillos as superintendent in split vote
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — Bustillos will join SAISD from a Houston school district. He said he's ready to prevent the school district from a state takeover. item.title: [$] Debating the role of large language models in the kernel community
item.description: [AI & Automation] Keyword: large language model — Like many development communities, the kernel community has been struggling to determine how large language models will be used in its development process. The news has been dominated recently by a strongly worded missive from Linus Torvalds on the subject, but the discussion has been rather more wide-ranging and nuanced than that. Topics that have been considered recently include the LLM attribution requirement, code-review tools, dependence on proprietary tools, and whether there is a place

item.title: AI music platform Suno hits bum note as 55M users exposed in data breach, claims infosec expert
item.description: [Cybersecurity] Keyword: data breach — Have I Been Pwned confirms scale for first time

item.description: [SAPD Calls for Service] Keyword: Burglary — Incident #SAPD-2026-0986490 | CENTRAL Division | 7/21/2026 8:13:02 AM

item.title: Triassic inferno: Fires raged across Europe for millennia during past global warming
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: global warming — The end-Triassic mass extinction, 201 million years ago, is linked to volcanism that resulted from the break-up of Pangea and which pumped out vast amounts of CO2 into the atmosphere, causing extreme global warming of 5 to 10 degrees Celsius. As a result, tree-dominated vegetation collapsed and the highly disturbed landscapes were colonized by ferns that formed savannahs across large swaths of what is now Northwest Europe.

item.title: How AI may drive union-resistant tech workers to the bargaining table
item.description: [Worker Cooperatives & Solidarity Economy] Keyword: collective bargaining — Tech workers are increasingly unionizing, trading Silicon Valley’s myth of exceptionalism for collective bargaining to contest the corporate deployment of artificial intelligenceFor decades, the technology industry was a fortress that labor unions couldn’t breach. Tech workers already had cushy compensation packages, dream benefits like unlimited vacation and free lunch, and a flat corporate hierarchy that made engineers feel as powerful as their bosses, all of whom dressed down in sneakers and

item.title: The Daily Reach and Recap have a whole new look: Sign up for our redesigned newsletters
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — The refresh aims to make readers a more digestible experience as they engage with San Antonio Report content.The Daily Reach and Recap have a whole new look: Sign up for our redesigned newsletters was first posted on July 21, 2026 at 8:00 am.©2021 "San Antonio Report". item.title: AI agent helps prepare synchrotron X-ray experimental measurements, paving the way for autonomous operation
item.description: [AI & Automation] Keyword: AI agent — Artificial intelligence (AI) models are now used daily by many people worldwide, both for professional and personal purposes. Over the past decades, scientists specialized in various disciplines have also started using these models to conduct research or simplify their experimental practices.

item.title: Tribes and Environmental Groups Sue to Block Trump’s Gutting of the Endangered Species Act
item.description: [Ecology & Conservation] Keyword: endangered species — This story was originally published by Inside Climate News and is reproduced here as part of the Climate Desk collaboration. Environmental groups and tribes are suing the Trump administration over a new rule eliminating the majority of habitat protections for imperiled wildlife protected by the Endangered Species Act.  Experts say the rule represents the most profound change to the law since it […]

item.title: Afghanistan: Deadly flash floods hit Nuristan province
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Some 20 people have been killed and more than 80 injured, with the city of Parun hit particularly hard. A lack of infrastructure and the effects of climate change have left the country vulnerable to natural disaster.

item.title: Indigenous knowledge key to building healthier and more sustainable food systems
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Traditional and Indigenous knowledge could play a vital role in addressing some of the world's most pressing challenges, including climate change, biodiversity loss and poor health and nutrition, according to a new review of current research.

item.title: VIA’s plans for Silver Line bus stops in historic districts head for review
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — The Historic and Design Review Commission is scheduled to consider the plans August 5 and make a recommendation for eight of the 18 stations. item.title: Trump cuts to advisory groups leaves hospitals without key expertise on disease
item.description: [Cannabis, Hemp & Drug Policy] Keyword: THCA — Under the Trump administration, dozens of advisory committees have been ended or frozen, including one on controlling infection in healthcare settings.

item.title: Global Value Chain Trade Reaches Historic Highs, Despite Push for Reshoring
item.description: [Worker Cooperatives & Solidarity Economy] Keyword: co-op — Despite rising geopolitical tensions, trade restrictions and calls to bring production back to domestic shores, the global economy remains more interconnected than it may appear. Trade through global value chains (GVCs) reached historic levels in 2024, according to a new report from the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD). GVCs, the cross-border production networks […]

item.title: Pentagon Slashed Civilian Harm Reduction Staff and Is Instead Using AI
item.description: [Opioid Treatment & Methadone] Keyword: harm reduction — CENTCOM, which oversees the war in Iran, has had its civilian harm reduction team slashed from 10 to just one.

item.title: Protect Your Privacy with California's DROP Tool
item.description: [Digital Rights & Privacy] Keyword: data broker — Are you a California resident? Then we've got exciting news for you: there's a tool just for you that lets you take a single, relatively easy step to protect your privacy. It's called a DROP request. (That's Delete Request and Opt-out Platform, if you're fancy). This one bit of paperwork lets you tell every data broker registered in the state of California that you'd like them to delete your information from their databases and request they stop selling and sharing your information. Here are s

item.title: [SAPD] Assault In Progress — 8000 MIDCROWN DR (EAST)
item.description: [SAPD Calls for Service] Keyword: Assault In Progress — Incident #SAPD-2026-0983625 | EAST Division | 7/20/2026 3:56:19 PM

item.title: Ancestral variation, not new mutations, may impact survival of species facing climate change
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Research co-authored by an Oklahoma Museum of Natural History curator, her graduate student and an international team of scientists has shed new light on the evolutionary "toolkit" animals use to survive environmental change.

item.title: New web tool maps fossil locations across ancient continents in seconds
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Calculating a fossil's position throughout geological history is a complex process. In paleontology and other disciplines, determining a location in the past is a challenge that requires refined computational tools to process large volumes of data. Now, the BIOST3 Research Group at the University of Barcelona has designed an open-access web interface for the general public that simplifies this process and facilitates access to high-quality paleogeographic reconstructions.

item.title: TxDOT ramps up Operation Slowdown, speeding enforcement across Texas
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — Operation Slowdown is back. Here’s why Texas drivers will see more speed enforcement and what TxDOT wants motorists to know.TxDOT ramps up Operation Slowdown, speeding enforcement across Texas was first posted on July 20, 2026 at 3:28 pm.©2021 "San Antonio Report". Use of this feed is for personal non-commercial use only. If you are not reading this article in your feed reader, then the site is guilty of copyright infringement. Please contact me at hello@rivardreport.com

item.title: Frontier LLMs couldn't help Hugging Face fight off evil agents
item.description: [AI & Automation] Keyword: LLM — Chinese open-weight model GLM 5.2 happily obliged

item.title: Move over quokkas—Rottnest's salt lake bacteria could be the island's next big claim to fame
item.description: [Science & Research] Keyword: genomics — Scientists at Murdoch University have read the full genetic code of bacteria from the salt lakes of Wadjemup (Rottnest Island), giving them their clearest picture yet of how these tiny organisms could be used to make biodegradable plastic. Their research is published in the journal Microbial Genomics.

item.title: Japan's first mosasaur premaxilla fossil hints at a new species
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — A research team from Okayama University of Science, Tokyo City University, the Natural History Museum, Kishiwada City (Osaka Prefecture), and other institutions has identified four previously unrecognized fossil bones from a rock containing mosasaur remains collected about 30 years ago in Kishiwada City, Osaka Prefecture.

item.title: NISD picks longtime employee Jessica Palomares as interim superintendent
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — Jessica Palomares is the assistant superintendent of elementary education. She first joined NISD in 1996 as a special ed teacher. NISD picks longtime employee Jessica Palomares as interim superintendent was first posted on July 20, 2026 at 1:11 pm.©2021 "San Antonio Report". Use of this feed is for personal non-commercial use only. If you are not reading this article in your feed reader, then the site is guilty of copyright infringement. item.title: Civil rights groups sue Trump administration over childcare funding restrictions
item.description: [Government Accountability] Keyword: FOIA — HHS failed to respond to Foia request following claims of fraud against Somali childcare providers and DemocratsCivil rights groups have sued the US Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) after the agency failed to respond to a Freedom of Information Act request regarding the Trump administration’s restrictions on federal childcare and family assistance following claims of fraud against Somali childcare providers and Democratic state officials.The American Civil Liberties Union, the ACLU

item.title: Antibiotic-resistant bacteria increasingly leaving children vulnerable to common infections, global study finds
item.description: [Diabetes & Kidney Disease] Keyword: dialysis — Exclusive: Research spanning 82 countries finds antibiotic resistance increased in every region between 2004 and 2022Harry Booth has had a rough run through Australia’s coldest months. The 13-year-old has been in and out of hospital with a range of common chest and urinary tract infections that his doctors find challenging to treat.Harry was born with kidney failure and began dialysis at six months old, before receiving a kidney transplant from his father at age four. The surgery saved his life

item.title: New machine-learning tool improves accuracy of genomics research
item.description: [Science & Research] Keyword: genomics — University of Virginia School of Medicine scientists have identified a widespread source of error in a popular method for studying the genome and created a machine-learning tool to correct it. The free tool could improve the reliability of both conventional and single-cell data generated using this method, giving researchers a clearer view of how gene activity is controlled in health and disease and providing stronger foundations for future diagnostic and drug-development efforts.

item.title: Earth’s waters are quietly running out of oxygen, scientists warn
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: biodiversity loss — Oxygen is disappearing from oceans, lakes, rivers, and coastal waters at an alarming rate, threatening aquatic life and weakening the natural processes that help regulate Earth’s climate. Scientists warn that this widespread deoxygenation is closely connected to other major planetary threats, including warming, pollution, biodiversity loss, and ocean acidification.

item.title: Nearly 1,000 Wildfires in Canada Continue to Burn as Trump & Carney Double Down on Fossil Fuels
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Millions of people across North America have experienced hazardous air pollution this month as nearly a thousand active wildfires continue to rage in Canada. In addition to releasing hazardous chemicals and smoke darkening skies across the continent, the fires in Canada have also disproportionately impacted Indigenous communities, forcing thousands to evacuate their homes. Republican politicians have been loath to acknowledge the role of the climate crisis in making extreme weather events like t

item.title: Double murder accused strangled Devon cellmate while awaiting trial, jury told
item.description: [AI & Automation] Keyword: LLM — James Desborough was convicted in March of murdering Steven Kempster, Winchester crown court jurors hearA fan of a TV show about serial killers strangled a cellmate in prison while awaiting trial for allegedly murdering two men and hiding their remains in Cornish woodland, a jury has heard.James Desborough, 40, is accused of killing Claudio Aquilino and Daniel Coleman and concealing parts of their bodies in the dense undergrowth close to his cabin near St Austell. Continue reading...
